name: wiki-pagination
description: Prev/Next article navigation within a wiki category. Three-column grid: prev article (left), category link (centre), next article (right). NOT numbered-page pagination. Keyboard navigable via native <a> elements.
research_notes: Zola sort semantics (training data, verify against getzola.org): sort_by field in _index.md controls section order.
Template variables: page.lower (previous in sort order), page.higher (next in sort order).
Example: {% if page.lower %}← {{ page.lower.title }}{% endif %}
Note: 'lower' and 'higher' refer to position in sort order, not alphanumeric comparison.
Open question: which sort_by is used by the wiki engine? Must be confirmed against project-knowledge _index.md files.
html: ← Previous {{prev-article-title}} {{category-name}} {{next-article-title}} Next →
layout: grid-template-columns: 1fr auto 1fr; compact breakpoint (≤799px): stack vertically.

variants.name: first-article
variants.description: No prev; spacer holds left cell.

variants.name: last-article
variants.description: No next; spacer holds right cell.

variants.name: only-article
variants.description: Both edges empty; category link only.

aria: <nav aria-label='Articles in {{category-name}}'>. aria-label on each <a> provides full context ('Previous article: {{title}}'). Arrow glyphs aria-hidden. rel=prev/rel=next on <a> elements; engine also emits <link rel> in <head> (engine responsibility).
open_questions: Confirm which sort_by value the wiki engine uses for category sections — determines whether page.lower/page.higher in Zola maps to expected editorial ordering.
